reason: This text (Genesis 4) recounts the story of Cain and Abel, focusing on individual actions and personal consequences. While it describes Cain's murder of Abel and God's resulting curse upon Cain (that he becomes a fugitive and the ground will not yield its strength to him), these are individual punishments directed at one person, not a prophecy of upcoming widespread destruction affecting the general public. There is no mention of any broader disaster, calamity, or public destruction by Yahweh in this passage.
